preamble: The vocalization of the voiced uvular fricative [ʁ] into the open central vowel [a] (or the near-open central vowel [ɐ]) is a common form of r-vocalization, particularly prevalent in Germanic languages when the consonant appears in the syllable coda.

examples.note: Standard vocalization of the uvular rhotic in coda position to a near-open central vowel.
Vocalization resulting in a centering diphthong.
Extensive vocalization of the uvular rhotic across various dialects and standard Danish.
